Name: adaptive_kernel_optimization
Title: Adaptive Profiling for Real-Time GPU Kernel Optimization in Machine Learning Inference
Short Hypothesis: Real-time adaptive profiling of GPU kernels can significantly enhance inference performance by optimizing execution based on dynamic workload characteristics, distinguishing it from static profiling methods that do not account for runtime variability.
Related Work: Existing work like Astra and TritonForge focuses on optimizing GPU kernels through predefined strategies or static analysis. In contrast, this proposal emphasizes real-time profiling to adaptively adjust kernel configurations based on live performance metrics, allowing for a more reactive optimization approach that can better handle workload fluctuations.
Abstract: In the era of machine learning applications, optimizing GPU kernel performance is crucial for achieving low-latency inference. Traditional optimization techniques often rely on static profiling, which may not effectively accommodate the dynamic nature of workloads. We propose a novel framework for adaptive profiling that continuously monitors GPU kernel performance in real time, leveraging metrics such as occupancy, memory bandwidth utilization, and kernel execution time. This framework dynamically adjusts kernel parameters, such as tile sizes and precision settings, based on the current workload and performance characteristics. By employing a systematic A/B testing approach, we will validate the effectiveness of this adaptive method against static profiling techniques across diverse machine learning models. Our evaluation metrics will include p50/p99 latency, throughput under varying batch sizes, and energy efficiency. We anticipate that this adaptive profiling strategy will yield significant performance improvements and provide a more robust solution for real-time machine learning inference, ultimately bridging the gap between static optimization techniques and the variability inherent in live workloads.
Experiments: Implement the adaptive profiling framework in a controlled environment using a commodity GPU.
Conduct A/B tests comparing the adaptive profiling method against static profiling approaches across several machine learning models (e.g., CNNs, Transformers).
Measure and report p50/p99 latency and throughput under various batch sizes to assess performance improvements.
Evaluate energy consumption per token processed to ensure improvements are not only in speed but also in efficiency.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the overhead introduced by real-time profiling, which may negate performance gains if not carefully managed. Additionally, the effectiveness of adaptive profiling may vary with different models and workloads, necessitating comprehensive evaluation across diverse scenarios.
